name: Excessive Web Server Traffic from Single Source (Potential Scan/Brute Force/C2)
description: Detects an unusually high number of HTTP/HTTPS requests from the same source IP within a short timeframe, indicating web application scanning, brute-force attacks, Command and Control (C2) activity, or DDoS/DoS reconnaissance.

parameters.name: Timestamp
parameters.description: The timestamp of one of the web request events (e.g., the most recent one).

parameters.name: SourceIp
parameters.description: The IP address of the client making the web request.

parameters.name: DestinationIp
parameters.description: The IP address of the web server.

parameters.name: HttpMethod
parameters.description: The HTTP method used (e.g., GET, POST).

parameters.name: UserAgent
parameters.description: The User-Agent string from the client.

parameters.name: ComputerName
parameters.description: The hostname of the web server.

parameters.name: RuleName
parameters.description: The name of the Sigma rule that triggered the event, if applicable.

parameters.name: SIEM_COUNT_OF_EVENTS
parameters.description: The aggregated count of web requests by the SIEM (e.g., 75).

parameters.name: SIEM_TIMEFRAME
parameters.description: The time window over which the events were counted (e.g., '1 minute').

parameters.name: SIEM_FIRST_EVENT_TIMESTAMP
parameters.description: The timestamp of the first event in the correlated group.

parameters.name: SIEM_LAST_EVENT_TIMESTAMP
parameters.description: The timestamp of the last event in the correlated group.
